stories.title: Isa Torres to Texas — What the Transfer Really Means for the Three-Peat
stories.angle: The portal is closed. Texas just got the Player of the Year. Isa Torres — .530 BA, .978 SLG, .591 OBP, 78 runs, NCAA record 16 consecutive at-bats with a hit — is headed home to her native Texas. A Georgetown, TX native joining the two-time defending national champions. She slots into a lineup that already has Katie Stewart (31 HRs, school record), Teagan Kavan, and All-Big 12 catcher Samantha Swan. With pitcher Madi Doty (No. 2 prospect nationally) also joining, the Longhorns are building something we may never see again.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
She hit .530. She slugged .978. She set an NCAA record with 16 straight at-bats on base.

Now Isa Torres is coming home to Texas.

Portal closed. Longhorns got better. 🤘 #LonghornSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Torres (.530 BA). Stewart (31 HRs). Swan (All-Big 12 catcher). Kavan.

Texas's 2027 lineup may be the most dangerous in college softball history.

Three-peat just got a lot more likely. #LonghornSoftball #IloveSoftball

facebook.long_form:
ISA TORRES IS A LONGHORN 🤘

The portal is closed. And the most dominant player in college softball is headed home.

Torres — the 2026 D1 Softball Player of the Year — signed with the two-time defending national champions. She hit .530 this season. She slugged .978. She posted a .591 on-base percentage and scored 78 runs. She set an NCAA record with 16 consecutive at-bats reaching base safely. A Georgetown, TX native, she's coming home after four seasons at Florida State.

She's joining a team that already won back-to-back national championships. And she's not the only addition.

The 2027 Texas lineup: Torres at short, Katie Stewart at the corner (school-record 31 HRs this year), Samantha Swan behind the plate (All-Big 12 catcher from Arizona State), and Teagan Kavan heading into her senior year. Pitcher Madi Doty — Softball America's No. 2 prospect nationally — joins the staff.

This is not a reload. This is a dynasty adding the best player in the sport while already on top.

Can anyone stop the Longhorns in 2027?
facebook.image_caption:
"I'm coming home." — Isa Torres commits to Texas 🤘

The 2026 D1 Player of the Year (.530 BA / .978 SLG / 78 runs) joins the two-time national champions. Portal closed. The three-peat is on.

stories.title: FSU Softball's Painful Summer — Losing Torres and Beachum, but the Seminoles Aren't Done
stories.angle: Florida State lost the two biggest names in the 2026 transfer portal — Isa Torres to Texas and Jaysoni Beachum to Florida. It was a brutal 15-day window for Tallahassee. But the Seminoles aren't starting from zero: ACC Pitcher of the Year Jazzy Francik returns. Two-way star Ashtyn Danley returns. New additions Nicole Edmiaston (Stetson) and Ella Dodge (Tennessee) combined for 34 HRs — more than Torres and Beachum together. FSU is also pursuing Oklahoma's Tia Milloy. The offseason hurt, but this program has rebuilt before.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
FSU lost Torres AND Beachum in the same portal window.

That stings. But Jazzy Francik (ACC Pitcher of the Year) returns. Danley returns. New additions combined for more HRs than the two stars who left.

The Seminoles aren't done. #FSUSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Torres to Texas. Beachum to Florida.

FSU's summer was brutal.

But Jazzy Francik is still in Tallahassee. Programs built on elite pitching don't stay down long.

Watch for the reload. #FSUSoftball #IloveSoftball

facebook.long_form:
There's no sugarcoating what happened to Florida State softball in the 2026 transfer portal window.

Isa Torres — D1 Player of the Year, .530 hitter, program all-time record holder in batting average and OBP — left for Texas. Jaysoni Beachum — Softball America's No. 1 transfer, .417 BA, 16 HR, 66 RBI — left for Florida. In a single 15-day window, the Seminoles lost two of the most decorated players in program history.

But here's the thing about FSU: they have rebuilt before.

ACC Pitcher of the Year Jazzy Francik is coming back. Two-way star Ashtyn Danley is coming back. The Seminoles added Nicole Edmiaston from Stetson and Ella Dodge from Tennessee — and those two combined for 34 home runs last season, more than Torres and Beachum combined (26).

Yes, Torres and Beachum brought more than just home runs. They drove runs, set the table, and anchored the lineup in ways stats don't fully capture. The losses are real. But the narrative that FSU is finished is premature.

Programs built on elite pitching survive roster turnover. Jazzy Francik is one of the best pitchers in the country. Head coach Lonni Alameda has rebuilt this program more than once. Count the Seminoles out at your own risk.
facebook.image_caption:
Torres to Texas. Beachum to Florida.

But Francik returns. Danley returns. FSU's new additions combined for more HRs than the two stars who left.

The Seminoles aren't finished. 🍢

article.headline: FSU Softball's Painful Summer: Losing Torres and Beachum, but the Seminoles Aren't Done

stories.title: Post-Portal Power Rankings — Who Really Won the 2026 Transfer Window?
stories.angle: The window is shut. 122 players entered, 89 committed (73%). Now we can score it: Texas assembled the most balanced class (Torres + Swan + Lincoln); Texas Tech assembled the most decorated class (Polar + Heavener + Pickering — three All-Americans); Florida made the single biggest acquisition (Beachum). FSU survived by retaining its pitching and adding power. Final power ranking for 2027: No. 1 Texas, No. 2 Texas Tech, No. 3 Florida, No. 4 Nebraska, No. 5 South Carolina.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
2027 Power Rankings — portal closed:

No. 1 Texas (Torres + Stewart + Swan)
No. 2 Texas Tech (Polar + Heavener + Pickering)
No. 3 Florida (Beachum + returning core)
No. 4 Nebraska
No. 5 South Carolina

Disagree? 👇 #Softball #IloveSoftball

facebook.long_form:
The portal is closed. 122 players entered. 89 committed (73%). The 15-day window is history.

Now we can finally score it.

🥇 No. 1 TEXAS — D1 Player of the Year Isa Torres. All-Big 12 catcher Samantha Swan. Pitcher Samantha Lincoln. Added to a lineup that already had Katie Stewart (31 HRs) and Teagan Kavan. They won two straight national championships and then found the best player in the portal. The Longhorns win on overall talent level.

🥈 No. 2 TEXAS TECH — The most All-American-loaded class in portal history. Kenna Polar (.555 BA / 101 hits from Purdue). Jayden Heavener (nation's top pitcher, 13-9 / 2.81 ERA from LSU). Kasidi Pickering (.370 BA / 20 HR from Oklahoma). Softball America's No. 1 class. Three consensus All-Americans. One WCWS chip on their shoulder.

🥉 No. 3 FLORIDA — One move, maximum impact. Jaysoni Beachum — Softball America's No. 1 transfer, .417 BA, 16 HR, 66 RBI — commits on the final day. Tim Walton fills the Grover vacancy and gives the Gators a genuine star to build around.

No. 4 NEBRASKA — Built depth across the roster quietly and effectively.

No. 5 SOUTH CAROLINA — Meaningful moves in the SEC without overspending their roster budget.

The biggest loss: Florida State. But with Francik and Danley returning, the Seminoles stay competitive even after a painful window.

Who do you think wins 2027? Drop your prediction below. 👇
facebook.image_caption:
Portal closed. Final scoreboard is in. 🏆

No. 1 Texas | No. 2 Texas Tech | No. 3 Florida | No. 4 Nebraska | No. 5 South Carolina

Tag a softball fan and debate it. 👇

stories.title: Texas vs. Texas Tech — The 2027 National Championship Preview Starts Now
stories.angle: The 2026 WCWS final was Texas 4-1 over Texas Tech in Game 2 (after a 7-3 Texas win in Game 1). The Red Raiders came within two wins of a title with the team they had. Now they have Kenna Polar (.555 BA), Jayden Heavener (nation's top pitcher), and Kasidi Pickering (.370 BA / 20 HR). Texas countered with Torres. Both programs appear to be on a collision course for Oklahoma City in 2027. The rematch no one can stop talking about may already be written.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
2026 WCWS Final: Texas over Texas Tech.

Texas Tech's response: Polar + Heavener + Pickering — three All-Americans.
Texas's response: Isa Torres, Player of the Year.

The 2027 rematch was written the day the portal opened. #LonghornSoftball #WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
On June 4, 2026, Texas beat Texas Tech 4-1 to win back-to-back national championships.

The Red Raiders went 61-8. They came within two wins of their first title. And instead of retreating, they went to work.

Kenna Polar (.555 BA, 101 hits, Purdue) — in the portal. Texas Tech signs her.
Jayden Heavener (nation's No. 1 pitcher, 13-9 / 2.81 ERA at LSU) — in the portal. Texas Tech signs her.
Kasidi Pickering (.370 BA / 20 HR / 61 RBI, Oklahoma, On3 No. 2 overall) — in the portal. Texas Tech signs her.

Softball America called it the No. 1 transfer class in the country. Three All-Americans to one school.

Texas's response? Sign Isa Torres — the 2026 D1 Player of the Year, .530 BA, .978 SLG, 78 runs — and bring her home to Austin.

This is what an arms race at the top of college softball looks like. Both programs watched the same 2026 WCWS final and came to the same conclusion: more.

The 2027 national championship game may already be written. Texas. Texas Tech. Oklahoma City.

Get ready. 📅
facebook.image_caption:
2026 WCWS Final: Texas over Texas Tech. 🏆

Texas Tech's response: THREE All-Americans.
Texas's response: the Player of the Year.

The 2027 rematch was written the day the portal opened. #Softball

stories.title: The Offseason Begins — What's Next for College Softball After the Portal Closed
stories.angle: The transfer portal is closed. The 2026 season is over. Texas is the champion. So what happens now? Summer showcases (Premier Girls Fastpitch, Top Gun, Triple Crown) begin evaluating the 2027 high school class. September 1 opens the contact window for 2028 prospects. Programs turning to fall practice in August. The NFCA Convention brings coaching changes. And the next transfer portal window opens after the 2027 WCWS. College softball never really stops.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Portal closed. ✅ WCWS done. ✅

What's next:
→ Summer showcases (PGF, Top Gun, Triple Crown)
→ Sept. 1 opens D1 contact for 2028 prospects
→ Fall practice starts in August
→ Next portal opens after 2027 WCWS

Softball never stops. #IloveSoftball

facebook.long_form:
The portal is closed. Texas is the champion. The 15-day window that reshaped 2027 college softball is officially over.

So what comes next?

Here's the offseason calendar for fans who want to stay connected:

☀️ RIGHT NOW — SUMMER SHOWCASES
Premier Girls Fastpitch, Top Gun, and Triple Crown circuits are the proving grounds for high school prospects. College coaches are in the stands evaluating who to pursue for the 2027 and 2028 recruiting classes. If you're a high school player, summer is the most important evaluation period of the year.

📅 SEPTEMBER 1 — RECRUITING CONTACT OPENS
The single most important date in softball recruiting. D1 coaches can initiate direct contact with rising juniors (class of 2028) starting September 1. This is when verbal offers, phone calls, and campus visit conversations begin in earnest.

🏟️ AUGUST — FALL PRACTICE BEGINS
College programs return to campus. Portal additions meet returning rosters for the first time. This is when Isa Torres and Katie Stewart first practice together in burnt orange. When Polar, Heavener, and Pickering first take the field at Texas Tech.

📋 FALL — NFCA CONVENTION
The softball coaching community gathers. Coaching changes often get announced here — hires, departures, staff restructuring. Worth watching for fans of programs that had busy offseasons.

🏆 AFTER 2027 WCWS — NEXT PORTAL WINDOW
The cycle repeats. The next transfer portal opens after the 2027 national championship. Whatever happens between now and then, the clock is already running on the next rebuild season.

College softball never truly stops. It just changes what it looks like.
facebook.image_caption:
Portal closed ✅ WCWS done ✅

The cycle rolls on: summer showcases → fall practice → 2027 recruiting.

What's next for your favorite program? 👇 #IloveSoftball
